candidates.evidence_quote: We have experienced significant disruptions to our business as federal, state and local restrictions have fluctuated over time to mitigate the spread of the COVID-19 virus
we incurred increased restaurant staff absenteeism and temporary shifts in consumer behavior
Sales through the off-premise channel comprised approximately 25% of our restaurant sales during fiscal 2022 as compared to 32% in fiscal 2021
we experienced labor shortfalls relative to our sales levels in certain parts of our workforce and/or geographies
we also began to experience certain supply shortages and transportation delays largely attributable to impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic
These shortages continued in fiscal 2022 and were exacerbated by geopolitical unrest
primarily due to inflation across most categories in excess of menu price increases (1.9%)
This increase was primarily due to wage rate inflation in excess of menu price increases (0.8%)
primarily due to an increase in comparable restaurant sales, reflecting the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic in fiscal 2021, as well as additional revenue related to new restaurant openings
Our overall revenue growth is primarily driven by revenues from new restaurant openings and increases in comparable restaurant sales
The increase from fiscal 2021 was primarily driven by increased customer traffic of 6.8%
an increase in average check of 0.2% (based on an increase of 5.9% in menu pricing, partially offset by a 5.7% negative change in mix)
We implemented effective menu price increases of approximately 3.25%, 4.25% and 2.8% in the first, third and fourth quarters of fiscal 2022, respectively
Average check variations are driven by menu price increases and/or changes in menu mix
North Italia comparable sales increased approximately 15% from fiscal 2021 and increased approximately 22% compared to fiscal 2019
Total operating weeks at The Cheesecake Factory restaurants increased 2.7% to 11,052 in fiscal 2022
As a percentage of revenues, food and beverage costs were 24.6% for fiscal 2022 compared to 22.3% for fiscal 2021
labor expenses, which include restaurant-level labor costs and bakery production labor, including associated fringe benefits, were 36.7% and 36.6% in fiscal 2022 and fiscal 2021
offset by a decrease in group medical insurance costs due to lower claim activity (0.8%) in fiscal 2022
increased restaurant-level incentive compensation expense (0.2%)
we recorded impairment of assets and lease terminations expense of $31.4 million primarily related to the impairment of long-lived assets

the fair value of the contingent consideration and compensation liability increased by $4.7 million due to a $8.3 million increase in the fair value primarily stemming from a change in the volatility factors
Preopening costs were $16.8 million for fiscal 2022 compared to $13.7 million for fiscal 2021
This decrease was primarily due to higher interest expense in the prior year ($3.5 million), mainly related to our interest rate swap
The change was due primarily to a higher proportion of employment credits in relation to income before income taxes (48.7%)
we had an income tax benefit of $10.2 million, an effective tax rate of (31.1%)
we anticipate total revenue for fiscal 2023 to be approximately $3.5 billion to $3.6 billion
we expect fiscal 2023 net income margin of approximately 4% at the mid-point of the estimated revenue range
We anticipate approximately $165 to $175 million in cash capital expenditures to support this level of unit development
Cash flows from operations decreased by $51.1 million from fiscal 2021 primarily due to lower net income, the timing of payroll disbursements
higher gift card redemptions and lower accrued incentive compensation
The increase is primarily due to the resumption of our quarterly dividend in the second quarter of fiscal 2022
We repurchased 2.0 million shares at a cost of $63.1 million during fiscal 2022
we had net availability for borrowings of $238.5 million, based on a $130.0 million outstanding debt balance and $31.5 million in standby letters of credit under the Revolver Facility
we issued $345.0 million in aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes
longer lead times in obtaining licenses and permits and in the timeline required to complete the overall leasing process with landlords

notes: Chunk contains one event: 10-K 0001104659-23-026072 part 5/6 (MD&A: results of operations, fiscal 2023 outlook, liquidity). No fiscal_kpis in this chunk.

Skipped (9): consumer-behavior shifts (too vague/bare category); menu-innovation strategy boilerplate (no causal claim); bakery/royalty revenue-stream strategy mention (descriptive only); Series A preferred stock conversion (single-instance capital event); interest-rate-swap termination (single-instance, June 2021); unit-level cash-on-cash return targets (strategy targets, not a causal event variable); CARES Act NOL carryback refund (instance-bound tax item); credit-facility covenant compliance statement (no causal claim); The Cheesecake Factory brand-level comparable sales (brand name is identical to the company legal name, banned in driver_name; company-wide comparable_restaurant_sales candidate covers it).
geopolitical_unrest is borderline-broad but is the source's named exacerbating cause of supply shortages; flag at reconcile if too vague.
north_italia_comparable_sales kept as brand-level driver separate from comparable_restaurant_sales per ontology R9.
